Paragraph

As a result, any \(\phi\) in \(L^{p}[a, b]\text{,}\) namely, an equivalence class of Cauchy sequences in \(C[a, b]\) under the \(L^{p}[a, b]\)-norm, has an \(a.e.\) pointwise defined realization defined on \([a, b]\text{.}\) We will denote such a realization by \(\phi (x)\text{.}\) In particular, if \(\phi\) in \(L^{p}[a, b]\) is such that \(\Vert \phi \Vert_{L^{p}[a, b]}=0\text{,}\) then \(\phi(x)=0\) \(a.e.\) on \([a, b]\text{.}\)
in-context
